stories.title: 'New York Times' alleges White House abuses legal system to intimidate reporters
stories.summary: The White House is facing a federal court hearing Thursday, where The New York Times will argue that the Trump administration is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters by subpoenaing them in a leak investigation related to a story about security concerns surrounding the new Air Force One jet, a gift from Qatar. The newspaper claims this action violates basic First Amendment protections for newsgathering activity. The case marks a significant escalation in the Trump administration's efforts to deter intense coverage of the president.

stories.title: Josh Duhamel Reveals How Life at Off-Grid Cabin Helped to Prepare Him for Rugged Role in 'Ransom Canyon'
stories.summary: Actor Josh Duhamel has revealed how his off-grid cabin in Minnesota helped prepare him for his role in the Netflix series "Ransom Canyon," where he plays ranch owner Staten Kirkland. The 53-year-old actor has owned a sprawling property in Minnesota for over a decade, transforming it into an idyllic retreat for his family. As Season 2 of the series approaches, Duhamel has highlighted the parallels between his real-life experiences and his on-screen character.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France has evacuated 20,000 people in the south-west as wildfires spread in the Gironde region, including 10,000 visitors from campsites overnight. About 3,100 hectares of land have been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon, with no injuries reported. Authorities warn conditions will worsen as temperatures rise and winds strengthen through the evening.

stories.title: Ford picks Apple Maps for its next EV platform and updated BlueCruise
stories.summary: Ford has chosen Apple Maps as the navigation system for its next electric vehicle platform, set to debut in 2027 with a $30,000 midsize pickup truck. The integration will provide drivers with turn-by-turn directions, real-time traffic updates, and intuitive search capabilities. This partnership will also power the next generation of Ford's BlueCruise driver-assist system, enabling hands-free highway driving.

stories.title: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands
stories.summary: President Donald Trump finalized a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia that drops longstanding US demands for Israel ties in exchange for civilian nuclear technology. The agreement, announced Thursday, reportedly ties the pact to Saudi participation in the Abraham Accords, though the deal’s text remains undisclosed. Critics warn the move could accelerate nuclear proliferation in the Middle East.

stories.title: Inquiry into Farage poster row at anti-racism show
stories.summary: An investigation is underway after Reform UK councillors allegedly pressured organisers to remove posters featuring Nigel Farage at a Wakefield anti-racism event on Saturday. Police and Wakefield Council confirmed they are examining the incident at the Love Music Hate Racism gathering at Wakefield Exchange. Organisers said two councillors threatened to shut down the event unless Farage’s image was taken down.

stories.title: Biennale Chief Slams EU for Withdrawing Funding Over Russia’s Presence at Art Exhibition During Film Festival Press Conference: ‘They Are Abandoning Us’
stories.summary: The European Union has withdrawn a €2 million grant from the Venice Biennale due to Russia's participation in the arts exhibition, marking the first time Russia has returned to the event since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Venice Biennale chairman Pietrangelo Buttafuoco has criticized the EU's decision, stating it undermines the autonomy and freedom of expression of the arts foundation. The withdrawn funds were intended for the Biennale's cinema sector activities, including the Biennale College film fund and the Venice Production Bridge initiatives.

stories.title: Australia open to playing Test against England in India
stories.summary: Australia's Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg has expressed openness to the idea of hosting a Test match against England in India, citing the country's strong relationship with the England and Wales Cricket Board and the Board of Control for Cricket in India. This would be a departure from the traditional home-and-away Ashes series, which is played every four years. A one-off Test in India could potentially generate significant interest in the format, with Cricket Australia already exploring opportunities to expand international cricket content in the region.

stories.title: Burnham acting like he's still mayor, says Badenoch
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Kemi Badenoch has accused Labour leader Andy Burnham of governing like he is still the mayor of Greater Manchester, citing a "spending spree" of early promises that lack clear funding. Burnham's policies include restoring a £2 cap on bus fares in England and cutting VAT on household electricity bills, with a total projected cost of around £1.8 billion. The government says it will fund these plans through a combination of savings and reallocation of existing budgets.

stories.title: Ghanaians' ICC petition over migrant attacks 'opportunistic', says South Africa
stories.summary: South Africa has dismissed as opportunistic a petition filed at the ICC by two Ghanaians seeking an investigation into attacks on foreigners. The petition, submitted last week, alleges crimes against humanity amid rising anti-migrant violence. South Africa insists its legal system can address the issue while diplomatic tensions with Ghana escalate.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study in JAMA Neurology links long-term road traffic noise to a higher Parkinson’s risk. For every 11.5-decibel increase in noise at home, the risk rose by 3% over 18 years among 3.1 million Danish adults. Researchers suggest quiet home areas may help reduce the effect.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel to finalize a new nuclear deal with the U.S. The agreement, which bans uranium enrichment for military use, hinges on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords. Saudi officials have previously insisted recognition would require a Palestinian state.

stories.title: Amazon gaming boss predicts future where players no longer need consoles
stories.summary: Amazon’s gaming chief Jeff Gattis predicts a future where players stream games online instead of buying consoles as tech costs rise. He made the remarks while announcing Amazon’s Luna cloud gaming service will launch inside the Prime Video app. The move aims to tap into a vast audience beyond traditional console owners.

stories.title: Codina on why she joined West Ham project
stories.summary: Spain defender Claudia Codina joined West Ham on Monday, citing Italian coach Rita Guarino’s clear vision as the key factor. The Women’s Super League side finished 10th last season and has strengthened with three new signings this summer. Codina aims to help West Ham build on their recent progress under Guarino.

stories.title: Help for electricity bills as crude oil nears $100
stories.summary: Alderney will apply up to £1.2 million in public funds directly to household electricity bills to offset rising crude oil prices nearing $100. The temporary subsidy targets residents reliant on diesel-generated power, unlike Guernsey and Jersey. The committee says this measure protects consumers while AEL pursues long-term fuel procurement deals.

stories.title: Crews at Reflecting Pool appear to gather evidence at failure points identified in Post report
stories.summary: Crews have gathered evidence at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, focusing on at least a dozen locations including three where peeling was previously documented. The examination comes after a Washington Post analysis identified these areas as failure points in the newly applied coating. Further investigation is expected to determine the cause of the peeling.

stories.title: Actress Jaime Winstone joins Strictly line-up
stories.summary: Actress Jaime Winstone joins the Strictly Come Dancing 2026 line-up as its eighth celebrity contestant. Known for roles in Made in Dagenham and EastEnders, Winstone called the ballroom challenge a "whole new world" but welcomed the chance to "get super sparkly." Rehearsals begin next month ahead of the autumn series.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Lobsang Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for 26 days, losing 11 kilograms, as protests against exam paper leaks continue in the country. The demonstrations have sparked public anger and posed a challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government. The protesters are demanding answers on why paper leaks happen and who benefits from them, with no sign of backing down.

stories.title: Arsenal sign Greek forward Tzolis for £34m
stories.summary: Arsenal have signed Greek forward Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge for £34 million on a long-term deal. The 24-year-old international arrives with 43 goals and 45 assists in 108 appearances for his former club. He will bolster Arsenal’s attacking options after the exit of Leandro Trossard to Besiktas earlier this month.

stories.title: Illegal waste investigation sees £275k forfeited
stories.summary: West Mercia Police have forfeited over £275,000 from three businesses under investigation for illegal waste activity across Herefordshire, Worcestershire and Shropshire. The force also seized six firearms, £78,000 in cash and a suspected stolen car during raids in Worcestershire. The Environment Agency confirmed it had taken action at multiple sites, with enforcement continuing as part of ongoing criminal probes.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024-25, 1,941 per 100,000 children from the most deprived areas were admitted compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation are key drivers, calling for urgent action to address the gap.

stories.title: BritBox’s ‘Gone’ Is a Masterfully Tense Crime Drama: TV Review
stories.summary: BritBox’s new crime drama Gone premieres today, adapting a true-crime novel into a tense mystery. The six-part series follows a detective hunting a serial killer across Scotland. Critics call it a gripping follow-up to Kay’s previous hits.

stories.title: ‘Why do I feel crappy?’: women are learning about their luteal phases - and Maha is weaponizing it
stories.summary: A growing number of women are tracking their luteal phases, the 14-day window between ovulation and menstruation, to understand mood swings and cravings. Health coach Emily Rae Detrick promotes this approach on a popular podcast tied to the "Make America Healthy Again" movement, framing it as honoring natural creation. Detrick warns that without pregnancy, women may experience heightened stress and irritability during this phase.

stories.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
stories.summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

stories.title: Why I Sought Out a Taliban Commander I Had Fought
stories.summary: First as a Marine and then as a journalist, I spent years tied to Afghanistan. I returned one last time to speak to a former enemy in search of answers, and perhaps closure.

stories.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
stories.summary: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

stories.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
stories.summary: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.
